headline: Understanding Why Your Workers’ Comp Claim for Tinnitus Was Rejected
articleBody: Tinnitus—that constant ringing in your ears—is often the first sign of permanent damage. Insurance companies hate paying for 'ringing' because they can't see it on a scan. You’ll need consistent medical records and proof of the decibel levels you worked in. Don't fight them alone if they say it's just 'old age.' Reach out to Just Work Comp Law to get the settlement you actually deserve.
